title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics
text: complete toolkit distribution dynamics R — analysing distribution values across units (regions, firms, markets, assets) evolves time, heading long run. sddr brings distribution-dynamics analysis one tidy, long-format framework pushes beyond existing package offers — continuous stochastic kernels, continuous-time transitions, modern inference current R (Python) tooling simply provide.

what: What makes sddr different — the new ideas
title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics
text: capabilities set sddr apart. (Status: ✅ available now · 🔜 active development.) 🔜 Continuous stochastic kernels — density-based distribution dynamics (Quah), estimating full conditional density arbitrary class binning. modern approach, almost absent R. 🔜 Continuous-time & irregular-interval Markov — panels observed regular time grid. 🔜 Modern inference — bootstrap Bayesian intervals transition probabilities, plus formal tests Markov order, time-stationarity, whether space matters . 🔜 Change-point / non-stationarity detection transition dynamics. 🔜 Simulation engine, animated & interactive visualisation, broom tidiers, optional compiled backend large panels.

title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics
text: complete, tidy, sf-friendly implementation classical toolkit: ✅ Classic Markov chains ergodic (steady-state) analysis ✅ Spatial Markov — transitions conditioned neighbourhood context 🔜 LISA / full-rank / geo-rank Markov · rank & exchange mobility (Tau family) · mobility indices (Prais, Shorrocks) · directional LISA · sequence analysis · mean first-passage & sojourn times long-format id / time / value data — transition-matrix bookkeeping.

title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("mqfarooqi1/sddr")

title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics
code: library(sddr)  df <- data.frame(   id    = rep(1:200, each = 5),   time  = rep(2000:2004, times = 200),   value = rnorm(1000) )  # Classic Markov chain over distribution quintiles. m <- markov(df, id = "id", time = "time", value = "value", k = 5) m steady_state(m)              # long-run distribution

title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics
text: sddr’s methods build directly distribution-dynamics literature — Quah (1993) distributional convergence Rey (2001) spatial Markov dynamics. correctness guarantee, methods overlap PySAL’s giddy (established reference implementation) sddr checked numerical parity: classic spatial Markov estimators currently reproduce giddy machine precision. sddr extends well past giddy R package griddy continuous-kernel, continuous-time, inference methods .

title: Spatial Distribution Dynamics

title: Full-rank Markov chain — full_rank_markov
text: Markov chain ranks rather discretised classes: unit's rank within cross-section (1 = highest value, n = lowest) becomes state, n n matrix records ranks transition one period next (Rey, 2014). Unlike markov() binning needed — full rank ordering retained.

code: full_rank_markov(data, id, time, value)

title: Full-rank Markov chain — full_rank_markov
text: data data frame long format one row per unit period. panel must balanced (every unit present every period). id, time, value Character scalars naming unit, period, numeric value columns.

title: Full-rank Markov chain — full_rank_markov
text: object class sddr_markov (n n rank transition matrix, counts, ergodic distribution).

title: Full-rank Markov chain — full_rank_markov
text: Rey, S. J. (2014). Fast algorithms space-time concordance measure. Computational Statistics, 29(3-4), 799-811.

title: Geographic-rank Markov chain — geo_rank_markov
text: Markov chain tracks, rank position, unit occupies time (Rey, 2016). States geographic units, n n matrix records units exchange rank positions — spatially explicit view positional mobility.

title: Geographic-rank Markov chain — geo_rank_markov
code: geo_rank_markov(data, id, time, value)

title: Geographic-rank Markov chain — geo_rank_markov
text: data data frame long format one row per unit period. panel must balanced (every unit present every period). id, time, value Character scalars naming unit, period, numeric value columns.

title: Geographic-rank Markov chain — geo_rank_markov

what: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
title: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
text: Discretises continuous longitudinal variable ordered classes estimates discrete-time, first-order Markov transition probability matrix together ergodic (steady-state) distribution. classic distribution-dynamics estimator Quah (1993): transition matrix summarises units move parts distribution one period next, steady-state distribution describes process settles long run. Input tidy, long-format panel data one row per unit-period. estimator agnostic units (regions, firms, households, ...) whether space involved; spatial conditioning added companion spatial_markov() function (available later release).

title: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
code: markov(data, id, time, value, k = 5, breaks = NULL, fixed = TRUE)

title: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
text: data data frame long format one row per unit period. id Name (character scalar) column identifying unit. time Name (character scalar) column giving period. Periods compared sorted order within unit. value Name (character scalar) numeric column analyse. k Integer number classes discretise value . Default 5 (quintiles). Ignored breaks supplied. breaks Optional numeric vector interior class boundaries. supplied fixed cut points used instead data-driven quantiles, giving length(breaks) + 1 classes. fixed Logical. TRUE (default) single set quantile breaks estimated pooled distribution applied every period (absolute mobility). FALSE, breaks re-estimated within period (relative mobility). Ignored breaks supplied.

title: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
text: object class sddr_markov: list elements matrix k k row-stochastic transition probability matrix P. transitions k k matrix observed transition counts. steady_state ergodic (stationary) distribution P. classes Number classes k. breaks class boundaries used (fixed). n Number units contributing least one transition. n_transitions Total number observed transitions. data input data augmented integer state column.

title: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
text: Quah, D. (1993). Empirical cross-section dynamics economic growth. European Economic Review, 37(2-3), 426-434. doi:10.1016/0014-2921(93)90031-3

title: Classic Markov chain for distribution dynamics — markov
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:50, each = 4),   time = rep(2000:2003, times = 50),   value = rnorm(200) ) m <- markov(df, id = "id", time = "time", value = "value", k = 4) m #> <sddr> classic Markov chain #>   units: 50 | transitions: 150 | classes: 4 | breaks: fixed #>  #> Transition probability matrix (rows = from, cols = to): #>       1     2     3     4 #> 1 0.225 0.225 0.275 0.275 #> 2 0.229 0.257 0.229 0.286 #> 3 0.306 0.333 0.111 0.250 #> 4 0.205 0.154 0.333 0.308 #>  #> Ergodic (steady-state) distribution: #>     1     2     3     4  #> 0.240 0.239 0.241 0.281  m$steady_state #>         1         2         3         4  #> 0.2396739 0.2387903 0.2408205 0.2807152

title: Mean first passage times — mfpt
text: mean first passage time class \(\) class \(j\) expected number periods unit currently \(\) first reach \(j\). Together transition matrix times summarise quickly distribution mixes. diagonal holds mean recurrence time \(1/\pi_j\) (expected return time class), \(\pi\) ergodic distribution. Computed Kemeny-Snell fundamental matrix \(Z = (- P + )^{-1}\), \(\) every row equal \(\pi\); \(M_{ij} = (\delta_{ij} - Z_{ij} + Z_{jj}) / \pi_j\).

title: Mean first passage times — mfpt
code: mfpt(x, ...)  # Default S3 method mfpt(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'sddr_markov' mfpt(x, ...)

title: Mean first passage times — mfpt
text: x row-stochastic matrix, object class sddr_markov. ... Currently unused.

title: Mean first passage times — mfpt
text: numeric matrix mean first passage times.

title: Mean first passage times — mfpt
text: Kemeny, J. G. Snell, J. L. (1976). Finite Markov Chains. Springer.

title: Markov mobility indices — mobility
text: Scalar summaries much mixing transition matrix implies — larger values mean mobility (faster-churning distribution), values near zero mean near-immobile system. Several classical indices provided: prais Prais index \((k - \mathrm{tr}(P)) / (k - 1)\) — average probability leaving class. determinant \(1 - |\det(P)|\). L2 \(1 - |\lambda_2|\), one minus second-largest eigenvalue modulus (mixing rate). shorrock1 \((k - k \sum_i \pi_i p_{ii}) / (k - 1)\), weighted initial distribution ini. shorrock2 \(\sum_i \sum_j \pi_i p_{ij} |- j| / (k - 1)\), penalising longer jumps.

title: Markov mobility indices — mobility
code: mobility(x, measure = "all", ini = NULL)

title: Markov mobility indices — mobility
text: x row-stochastic matrix, object class sddr_markov. measure One "prais", "determinant", "L2", "shorrock1", "shorrock2", "" (default) return every index. ini Optional initial distribution (length k) Shorrock indices; defaults uniform.

title: Markov mobility indices — mobility
text: named numeric vector requested index indices.

title: Markov mobility indices — mobility
text: Prais, S. J. (1955). Measuring social mobility. JRSS , 118(1), 56-66. Shorrocks, . F. (1978). measurement mobility. Econometrica, 46(5), 1013-1024. doi:10.2307/1911433

title: Markov mobility indices — mobility
code: P <- matrix(c(0.7, 0.2, 0.1,               0.2, 0.6, 0.2,               0.1, 0.3, 0.6), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) mobility(P) #>       prais determinant          L2   shorrock1   shorrock2  #>   0.5500000   0.8100000   0.4381966   0.5500000   0.2166667  mobility(P, "prais") #> prais  #>  0.55

title: sddr: Spatial Distribution Dynamics — sddr-package
text: tidy toolkit distribution dynamics: analysing cross-sectional distribution values evolves time settles long run. Provides discrete-time spatial Markov transition estimation ergodic (steady-state) analysis, extended rank-mobility measures, continuous stochastic kernels, continuous-time transitions modern inference. Methods build distribution-dynamics literature (Quah (1993) doi:10.1016/0014-2921(93)90031-3 ; Rey (2001) doi:10.1111/j.1538-4632.2001.tb00444.x ), use long-format 'id'/'time'/'value' data rather transition matrices, checked numerical parity reference 'giddy' library.

title: sddr: Spatial Distribution Dynamics — sddr-package
text: Maintainer: Muhammad Farooqi mqfarooqi@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Muhammad Farooqi mqfarooqi@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Sojourn times — sojourn_time
text: expected number consecutive periods unit remains class leaving , \(1 / (1 - p_{ii})\) class \(\). Large values indicate "sticky" classes strong persistence.

title: Sojourn times — sojourn_time
code: sojourn_time(x, ...)  # Default S3 method sojourn_time(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'sddr_markov' sojourn_time(x, ...)

title: Sojourn times — sojourn_time
text: x row-stochastic matrix, object class sddr_markov. ... Currently unused.

title: Sojourn times — sojourn_time
text: named numeric vector sojourn times (one per class). Absorbing classes (\(p_{ii} = 1\)) return Inf.

what: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
title: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
text: Estimates spatial Markov chain (Rey, 2001): set class-transition probability matrices, conditioned spatial context unit start period. markov() asks "given unit's class, move next?", spatial_markov() asks "given unit's class class spatial neighbourhood, move next?". Comparing conditional matrices pooled (aspatial) matrix reveals whether, , spatial context shapes distribution dynamics. neighbourhood summarised spatial lag: (row-standardised) weighted average neighbours' values. Supply either spatial weights matrix via weights (lag computed , per period), precomputed spatial-lag column via lag (keeping estimator independent particular spatial-data stack).

title: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
code: spatial_markov(   data,   id,   time,   value,   weights = NULL,   lag = NULL,   k = 5,   m = k,   breaks = NULL,   lag_breaks = NULL,   fixed = TRUE,   row_standardize = TRUE )

title: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
text: data data frame long format one row per unit period. id Name (character scalar) column identifying unit. time Name (character scalar) column giving period. Periods compared sorted order within unit. value Name (character scalar) numeric column analyse. weights Optional square numeric spatial weights matrix whose row column names match unit ids. Used compute spatial lag value within period. Requires balanced panel. Exactly one weights lag must supplied. lag Optional name (character scalar) column giving precomputed spatial lag value. k Integer number classes discretise value . Default 5 (quintiles). Ignored breaks supplied. m Integer number spatial-lag classes condition . Defaults k. breaks Optional numeric vector interior class boundaries. supplied fixed cut points used instead data-driven quantiles, giving length(breaks) + 1 classes. lag_breaks Optional numeric vector interior class boundaries spatial lag (lag analogue breaks). supplied, fixed cut points used instead data-driven quantiles. Supplying breaks lag_breaks makes classification fully explicit, exact agreement giddy (cutoffs / lag_cutoffs) obtained. fixed Logical. TRUE (default) single set quantile breaks estimated pooled distribution applied every period (absolute mobility). FALSE, breaks re-estimated within period (relative mobility). Ignored breaks supplied. row_standardize Logical; row-standardise weights computing lag (default TRUE). Ignored lag supplied.

title: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
text: object class sddr_spatial_markov: list elements matrices Length-m list k k conditional transition probability matrices, one per spatial-lag class. transitions Length-m list conditional count matrices. pooled k k pooled (aspatial) transition matrix; equals matrix markov() class definition. pooled_transitions Pooled count matrix. steady_states Length-m list conditional ergodic distributions (NA conditional matrix unvisited classes). classes, lag_classes k m. n, n_transitions Units contributing, total transitions. data Input augmented lagval, value state lag state.

title: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
text: Rey, S. J. (2001). Spatial empirics economic growth convergence. Geographical Analysis, 33(3), 195-214. doi:10.1111/j.1538-4632.2001.tb00444.x

title: Spatial Markov chain for distribution dynamics — spatial_markov
code: set.seed(1) n <- 12; periods <- 8 # Ring contiguity: each unit neighbours the next and previous on a circle. W <- matrix(0, n, n, dimnames = list(1:n, 1:n)) for (i in 1:n) {   W[i, i %% n + 1] <- 1   W[i, (i - 2) %% n + 1] <- 1 } df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(periods), function(p)   data.frame(id = 1:n, time = p, value = rnorm(n)))) sm <- spatial_markov(df, "id", "time", "value", weights = W, k = 3) sm #> <sddr> spatial Markov chain #>   units: 12 | transitions: 84 | value classes: 3 | lag classes: 3 | breaks: fixed #>  #> Pooled transition matrix (aspatial): #>       1     2     3 #> 1 0.267 0.367 0.367 #> 2 0.500 0.214 0.286 #> 3 0.231 0.423 0.346 #>  #> Conditional on spatial-lag class 1 (31 transitions): #>       1     2     3 #> 1 0.308 0.154 0.538 #> 2 0.700 0.200 0.100 #> 3 0.375 0.250 0.375 #>  #> Conditional on spatial-lag class 2 (29 transitions): #>       1     2     3 #> 1 0.333 0.556 0.111 #> 2 0.100 0.300 0.600 #> 3 0.100 0.600 0.300 #>  #> Conditional on spatial-lag class 3 (24 transitions): #>       1     2     3 #> 1 0.125 0.500 0.375 #> 2 0.750 0.125 0.125 #> 3 0.250 0.375 0.375

what: Ergodic (steady-state) distribution of a Markov chain — steady_state
title: Ergodic (steady-state) distribution of a Markov chain — steady_state
text: Computes long-run stationary distribution row-stochastic transition matrix, .e. probability vector \(\pi\) satisfying \(\pi P = \pi\) \(\sum_i \pi_i = 1\). obtained left eigenvector P associated eigenvalue closest 1.

title: Ergodic (steady-state) distribution of a Markov chain — steady_state
code: steady_state(x, ...)  # Default S3 method steady_state(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'sddr_markov' steady_state(x, ...)

title: Ergodic (steady-state) distribution of a Markov chain — steady_state
text: x row-stochastic matrix, object class sddr_markov. ... Currently unused.

title: Ergodic (steady-state) distribution of a Markov chain — steady_state
text: named numeric vector giving stationary distribution.

title: Ergodic (steady-state) distribution of a Markov chain — steady_state
code: P <- matrix(c(0.8, 0.2,               0.3, 0.7), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) steady_state(P) #>   1   2  #> 0.6 0.4

title: Kendall's tau rank correlation — tau
text: Kendall's \(\tau\) measures rank concordance two variables — distribution dynamics, typically set units' values two points time. (concordant minus discordant) pair count, normalised, natural summary positional (exchange) mobility: \(\tau = 1\) means ranks perfectly preserved, \(\tau = -1\) complete reversal, \(\tau \approx 0\) extensive rank shuffling. Ties handled tau-b correction.

title: Kendall's tau rank correlation — tau
code: tau(x, y)

title: Kendall's tau rank correlation — tau
text: x, y Numeric vectors equal length.

title: Kendall's tau rank correlation — tau
text: object class sddr_tau: list statistic tau, concordant discordant pair counts, asymptotic (normal) two-sided p_value, n.

title: Kendall's tau rank correlation — tau
text: Kendall, M. G. (1938). new measure rank correlation. Biometrika, 30(1/2), 81-93. doi:10.2307/2332226

title: Kendall's tau rank correlation — tau
code: set.seed(1) x <- rnorm(30) y <- x + rnorm(30)          # correlated ranks tau(x, y) #> <sddr> Kendall's tau #>   tau = 0.5126  (concordant 329, discordant 106, n = 30) #>   asymptotic two-sided p-value = 6.934e-05

what: Local Kendall's tau (local indicator of mobility association) — tau_local
title: Local Kendall's tau (local indicator of mobility association) — tau_local
text: Decomposes Kendall's tau per-observation contribution. unit's local tau average sign pairwise rank concordance units, \(\sum_{j \ne } S_{ij} / (n - 1)\), \(S_{ij} = \mathrm{sign}((x_i - x_j)(y_i - y_j))\). Units local tau near 1 keep relative position; near -1 move grain. overall (tau-) statistic mean -diagonal S.

title: Local Kendall's tau (local indicator of mobility association) — tau_local
code: tau_local(x, y)

title: Local Kendall's tau (local indicator of mobility association) — tau_local
text: x, y Numeric vectors equal length.

title: Local Kendall's tau (local indicator of mobility association) — tau_local
text: object class sddr_tau_local: list per-observation tau_local vector, global tau (tau-), sign matrix S, n.

title: Local Kendall's tau (local indicator of mobility association) — tau_local
Theta rank-mobility statistic — theta
text: Theta statistic (Rey, 2004) measures much overall rank movement system within-regime exchange. period--period transition sum regimes absolute net within-regime rank change, divided total absolute rank change. Values near zero indicate rank movement dominated within-regime shuffling nets ; larger values indicate coherent regime-level movement.

title: Theta rank-mobility statistic — theta
code: theta(y, regime)

title: Theta rank-mobility statistic — theta
text: y numeric matrix values, units rows time periods columns (least two columns). regime vector length nrow(y) assigning unit regime.

title: Theta rank-mobility statistic — theta
text: object class sddr_theta: list theta (one value per period--period transition), per-period total absolute rank change, column rank matrix ranks.

title: Theta rank-mobility statistic — theta
text: Rey, S. J. (2004). Spatial dependence evolution regional income distributions. Spatial Econometrics Spatial Statistics, 194-213.

title: Theta rank-mobility statistic — theta
code: set.seed(1) y <- matrix(rnorm(40), nrow = 10)    # 10 units, 4 periods regime <- rep(c("a", "b"), each = 5) theta(y, regime) #> <sddr> Theta rank-mobility statistic #>   2 regimes | 3 period transitions #>   theta by transition: #> [1] 0.1429 0.6364 0.3750

title: sddr 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial scaffold. markov(): classic discrete-time, first-order Markov transition estimation tidy long-format id/time/value panel data, quantile-based (fixed per-period) class discretisation. full_rank_markov() geo_rank_markov(): rank-based Markov chains (ranks--states, units-exchanging-rank-positions) — binning required. spatial_markov(): spatial Markov chain (Rey 2001) transition matrices conditioned spatial-lag class neighbourhood. Accepts either spatial weights matrix (lag computed internally) precomputed lag column, explicit breaks / lag_breaks cut points. matching cut points reproduces PySAL giddy’s spatial Markov matrices machine precision. steady_state(): ergodic / stationary distribution transition matrix sddr_markov object. mfpt(): mean first passage times (Kemeny-Snell fundamental matrix), mean recurrence times diagonal. sojourn_time(): expected persistence time class. tau(): Kendall’s tau rank correlation (positional / exchange mobility), concordant/discordant counts asymptotic p-value. theta(): Theta rank-mobility statistic (Rey 2004) decomposing rank change regime. tau_local(): per-observation (local) Kendall’s tau decomposition. mobility(): Markov mobility indices (Prais, determinant, second-eigenvalue L2, Shorrocks B1/B2 indices). print() methods sddr_markov sddr_spatial_markov. Hex logo, pkgdown site configuration, GitHub Actions R-CMD-check CI.
